questions.text: Sound is produced by ________ objects.
questions.explanation: Vibrations create pressure variations in the air which we hear as sound.

questions.text: Sound waves in air are ________ waves.
questions.explanation: Particles move parallel to the direction of wave propagation.

questions.text: The number of oscillations per unit time is called:
questions.explanation: Frequency is measured in Hertz (Hz).

questions.text: The distance between two consecutive compressions or rarefactions is:
questions.explanation: Wavelength (λ) is the spatial period of the wave.

questions.text: Sound travels fastest in:
questions.explanation: Particles are closer in solids, allowing faster transfer of vibrations.

questions.text: The range of audible frequency for humans is:
questions.explanation: Sounds below 20Hz (infrasonic) and above 20kHz (ultrasonic) are not audible to humans.

questions.text: The sensation of sound persists in our brain for about:
questions.explanation: This persistence of hearing is why we need a 0.1s gap to distinguish an echo.

questions.text: SONAR stands for:
questions.explanation: It uses ultrasound to detect underwater objects or sea depth.

questions.text: Loudness of sound depends on its:
questions.explanation: Greater amplitude means higher energy and louder sound.

questions.text: A stethascope works on the principle of:
questions.explanation: Sound reaches the doctor's ears through multiple reflections in the tube.

questions.text: Sound cannot travel through ________.
questions.explanation: Sound is a mechanical wave and requires a medium to propagate.

questions.text: The speed of sound in air (at 0°C) is approximately:
questions.explanation: Speed of sound increases with temperature; at room temperature it is ~344 m/s.

questions.text: A high-pressure region in a longitudinal wave is called a ________.
questions.explanation: Compressions are where particles are crowded together.

questions.text: The pitch of a sound depends on its ________.
questions.explanation: Higher frequency sounds have a higher pitch (shrillness).

questions.text: The SI unit of frequency is ________.
questions.explanation: Named after Heinrich Hertz; 1 Hz = 1 oscillation per second.

questions.text: To hear a distinct echo, the minimum distance of the obstacle from the source should be:
questions.explanation: Calculated based on speed of sound (344m/s) and persistence of hearing (0.1s).

questions.text: Sounds of frequency higher than 20 kHz are called ________.
questions.explanation: Ultrasound is used in medical scans and industry.

questions.text: Which part of the human ear converts sound vibrations into electrical signals?
questions.explanation: The cochlea in the inner ear sends signals via the auditory nerve to the brain.

questions.text: The distance between a compression and the next rarefaction is ________.
questions.explanation: One wavelength (λ) is the distance between two consecutive compressions.

questions.text: Reverberation is the ________ of sound.
questions.explanation: Persistence of sound in a hall due to repeated reflections.

questions.text: The quality (timbre) of sound helps us distinguish between ________.
options: Two sounds of same pitch and loudness
questions.explanation: Timbre depends on the shape of the sound wave produced by different sources.

questions.text: The speed of sound ________ with increase in temperature.
questions.explanation: Higher temperature gives more kinetic energy to particles, facilitating faster wave transfer.

questions.text: Speed of sound 'v', frequency 'ν' and wavelength 'λ' are related as:
questions.explanation: Speed = Distance / Time = Wavelength / Time Period = Wavelength * Frequency.

questions.text: Which of the following can produce infrasonic sound?
questions.explanation: Whales and elephants produce sounds below 20 Hz to communicate over long distances.

questions.text: The auditory range of human ear is:
questions.explanation: Frequencies outside this range are inaudible to normal humans.

questions.text: Sound travels faster in ________.
questions.explanation: Sound speed: Solids > Liquids > Gases.

questions.text: Which device uses ultrasound for cleaning parts located in hard-to-reach places?
questions.explanation: High frequency vibrations detach dust and grease particles.

questions.text: The persistence of sound in a big hall is called:
questions.explanation: This can be reduced by using sound-absorbent materials like heavy curtains.

questions.text: The magnitude of the maximum disturbance in the medium is called ________.
questions.explanation: Amplitude determines the loudness of the sound.

questions.text: A key of mechanical piano is struck gently and then struck harder. In second case:
options: Sound is louder, amplitude is more
Sound is louder, pitch is more
questions.explanation: Harder strike imparts more energy, increasing amplitude and thus loudness.

questions.text: The time taken for one complete oscillation is:
questions.explanation: Time period (T) is the reciprocal of frequency (1/f).

questions.text: Bats detect obstacles by receiving the ________.
questions.explanation: Bats use echolocation to navigate in the dark.

questions.text: Which of the following is not a mechanical wave?
questions.explanation: Light is an electromagnetic wave and doesn't need a medium.

questions.text: Loudness is measured in:
questions.explanation: The decibel scale is logarithmic and used for sound intensity levels.

questions.text: Sound is a form of ________.
questions.explanation: Sound is energy produced by vibrations that travel as waves.

questions.text: The visible part of the human ear is called:
questions.explanation: The pinna collects sound from the surroundings.

questions.text: Sound travels in air through a series of ________.
questions.explanation: Air particles move back and forth along the direction of propagation.

questions.text: Why do we see lightning before hearing thunder?
options: Human eyes are faster than ears
questions.explanation: Light speed (~3x10⁸ m/s) is vastly higher than sound speed (~344 m/s).

questions.text: The small bones in the middle ear are:
questions.explanation: They amplify the pressure variations received by the eardrum.

questions.text: Infrasonic sound has frequency ________.
questions.explanation: Infrasound is below the human hearing threshold.
